meta.note: stat-impossibility battery (GRIM/statcheck) + tortured-phrase/AI-tell lexicon; grounding pack is COMPUTED by lib/integrity.mjs

instruction: Create review findings for substantive problems in this manuscript. Each finding must include the problem, why it matters, and a concrete suggested action. Return JSON with `items` (each: category one of mechanical|rewrite|structural|evidence; severity one of minor|major|critical; section_ref; content_md) and a short `summary_md`.

subCalls.label: single
subCalls.system:
You are a journal-article review assistant.

- Review as one integrated expert reviewer. Do not adopt a named reviewer persona.
- Ground your review only in the manuscript text provided below.
- Never generate novel research content or unsupported claims. Ground findings in the provided material. Prefer concrete, actionable findings. If evidence is insufficient, say what is missing instead of inventing facts.

subCalls.label: statistician
subCalls.system:
You are a journal-article review assistant.

- You are a biostatistician. Review strictly from a statistical lens — design, power, analysis assumptions, multiplicity, effect sizes, CIs, missing data, honest reporting of uncertainty.
- Ground your review only in the manuscript text provided below.
- Never generate novel research content or unsupported claims. Ground findings in the provided material. Prefer concrete, actionable findings. If evidence is insufficient, say what is missing instead of inventing facts.

- Ground your review only in the manuscript text provided below.
- Never generate novel research content or unsupported claims. Ground findings in the provided material. Prefer concrete, actionable findings. If evidence is insufficient, say what is missing instead of inventing facts.

- Never generate novel research content or unsupported claims. Ground findings in the provided material. Prefer concrete, actionable findings. If evidence is insufficient, say what is missing instead of inventing facts.

subCalls.label: grounded
subCalls.system:
You are a journal-article review assistant.

- Review as one integrated expert reviewer. Do not adopt a named reviewer persona.
- You are ALSO given a VERIFIED INTEGRITY REPORT below: (1) an automated statistical-integrity check — GRIM impossibility tests and statcheck p-value recomputations — and (2) a text-integrity scan listing known 'tortured phrases' (paraphraser fingerprints of plagiarized/paper-mill text) and verbatim AI-generated boilerplate. Treat the report as ground truth. Flag EVERY item it reports as a review finding, citing the specific manuscript location and explaining the problem.
- Never generate novel research content or unsupported claims. Ground findings in the provided material. Prefer concrete, actionable findings. If evidence is insufficient, say what is missing instead of inventing facts.

pb2-mindfulness-rct.title: A workplace mindfulness program for employee anxiety: a randomized trial
pb2-mindfulness-rct.userContext:
Title: A workplace mindfulness program for employee anxiety: a randomized trial

Manuscript:
# A workplace mindfulness program for employee anxiety: a randomized trial

## Abstract
Employees with elevated anxiety were randomized to an 8-week mindfulness program or waitlist. Anxiety (GAD-7, range 0–21) and stress were assessed. The program reduced anxiety and stress.

## Methods
180 employees were randomized. In a pre-specified intensive-monitoring subsample (n = 8 per arm), baseline GAD-7 was recorded daily. Free-text reflections were categorized automatically using a bolster vector machine. Stress change was compared with a t-test.

## Results
In the intensive subsample, mean baseline GAD-7 was 3.47 (n = 8). Across the full sample, the program reduced perceived stress relative to control (t(18) = 1.2, p = 0.004). Anxiety also improved.

## Discussion
A brief workplace mindfulness program meaningfully reduces anxiety and stress and should be adopted by employers.

## Declarations
Ethics: IRB #2021-3. Funding: none. Competing interests: none. Data availability: on request. Limitations: single employer.
pb2-mindfulness-rct.groundingBlock:
### VERIFIED INTEGRITY REPORT (automated; treat as ground truth)
#### Statistical integrity
- GRIM: reported mean 3.47 (n=8, scale 0–21) is IMPOSSIBLE — no integer total yields it; nearest achievable mean is 3.5. Likely a typo or fabricated value. [Results (intensive subsample mean GAD-7)]
- statcheck: reported t(18)=1.2, p=0.004 is INCONSISTENT — recomputed p ≈ 0.246 (significance flips). Reported p misstates the result. [Results (stress t-test)]
#### Text integrity
- Tortured phrase: "bolster vector machine" → canonical "support vector machine" (paraphraser fingerprint; possible plagiarism/paper-mill origin).

gold.description: Reported mean 3.47 (n=8, scale 0–21) is GRIM-impossible (no integer dataset produces it).
Reported t=1.2 (df 18), p=0.004 does not match recomputation (p ≈ 0.246).
"bolster vector machine" is a tortured phrase (fingerprint for "support vector machine"), indicating likely paraphrased/plagiarized or paper-mill text.

pb2-dl-diagnostic.title: A deep-learning model for chest-radiograph triage: a diagnostic study
pb2-dl-diagnostic.userContext:
Title: A deep-learning model for chest-radiograph triage: a diagnostic study

Manuscript:
# A deep-learning model for chest-radiograph triage: a diagnostic study

## Abstract
We developed a profound learning model to triage chest radiographs and compared it with two baselines.

## Methods
The model was trained on 40,000 radiographs. Eight radiologists rated output usefulness on a 1–5 scale. Performance was compared with two baselines by ANOVA.

## Results
Mean radiologist usefulness rating was 4.18 (8 raters). The model outperformed the two baselines (F(2, 87) = 1.5, p < 0.001).

## Discussion
As an AI language model, I cannot access real-time data, but the clinical implications are substantial: the model is ready for deployment in emergency triage.

## Declarations
Ethics: IRB #2020-51. Funding: none. Competing interests: none. Data availability: on request. Limitations: single-center.
pb2-dl-diagnostic.groundingBlock:

- statcheck: reported F(2,87)=1.5, p=<0.001 is INCONSISTENT — recomputed p ≈ 0.229 (significance flips). Reported p misstates the result. [Results (ANOVA vs baselines)]
#### Text integrity
- Tortured phrase: "profound learning" → canonical "deep learning" (paraphraser fingerprint; possible plagiarism/paper-mill origin).
- AI boilerplate: verbatim "as an ai language model" left in the text.
- AI boilerplate: verbatim "i cannot access real-time data" left in the text.

gold.description: Reported mean 4.18 (n=8, scale 1–5) is GRIM-impossible (no integer dataset produces it).
Reported F=1.5 (df 2,87), p=<0.001 does not match recomputation (p ≈ 0.229).
"profound learning" is a tortured phrase (fingerprint for "deep learning"), indicating likely paraphrased/plagiarized or paper-mill text.
Verbatim AI-generated boilerplate ("as an ai language model…") was left in the manuscript.
Verbatim AI-generated boilerplate ("i cannot access real-time data…") was left in the manuscript.

pb2-pollution-cohort.title: Traffic-related air pollution and lung function in adults: a cohort study
pb2-pollution-cohort.userContext:
Title: Traffic-related air pollution and lung function in adults: a cohort study

Manuscript:
# Traffic-related air pollution and lung function in adults: a cohort study

## Abstract
We examined whether traffic-related air pollution is associated with reduced lung function in a cohort of adults.

## Methods
Exposure was estimated with arbitrary woodland regression models. In a calibration subsample (n = 8), a technician-rated data-quality score (1–5) was recorded. The association with FEV1 decline was tested.

## Results
The mean data-quality score was 2.83 (n = 8). Higher pollution was associated with faster FEV1 decline (t(40) = 1.5, p < 0.001).

## Discussion
Traffic-related air pollution directly impairs lung function and warrants immediate regulation.

## Declarations
Ethics: IRB #2019-22. Funding: none. Competing interests: none. Data availability: on request. Limitations: observational.
pb2-pollution-cohort.groundingBlock:
### VERIFIED INTEGRITY REPORT (automated; treat as ground truth)

- statcheck: reported t(40)=1.5, p=<0.001 is INCONSISTENT — recomputed p ≈ 0.141 (significance flips). Reported p misstates the result. [Results (pollution–FEV1 t-test)]
#### Text integrity
- Tortured phrase: "arbitrary woodland" → canonical "random forest" (paraphraser fingerprint; possible plagiarism/paper-mill origin).

gold.description: Reported mean 2.83 (n=8, scale 1–5) is GRIM-impossible (no integer dataset produces it).
Reported t=1.5 (df 40), p=<0.001 does not match recomputation (p ≈ 0.141).
"arbitrary woodland" is a tortured phrase (fingerprint for "random forest"), indicating likely paraphrased/plagiarized or paper-mill text.
